en_def: a deep, wide crack or gap in the ground; a very great difference between people, groups, or things
zh_def: 裂縫；深壑；（比喻）巨大的鴻溝
en_example: Think of Chaos as a kind of grand cosmic yawn, as in a yawning chasm or a yawning void.
en_example_chunks: Think of Chaos / as a kind of grand cosmic yawn, / as in a yawning chasm / or a yawning void.
zh_example: 把混沌想像成一種宇宙級的呵欠，就像一道張得老大的深壑或虛空。

en_def: a completely empty space
en_example: In the beginning there was nothing but a formless void, waiting to be filled.
en_example_chunks: In the beginning / there was nothing / but a formless void, / waiting to be filled.
zh_example: 在最初，除了一片渾沌虛空外一無所有，靜待著被填滿。

en_def: a gradual decline into disorder; a measure of the disorder in a system
en_example: It calls this inevitable fate entropy: part of the great cycle from Chaos to order and back again to Chaos.
en_example_chunks: It calls this inevitable fate entropy: / part of the great cycle / from Chaos to order / and back again to Chaos.
zh_example: 科學稱這個無可避免的命運為「熵」：從混沌到秩序，再度回歸混沌的巨大循環的一部分。

en_def: come together to form one mass or whole
en_example: Your trousers began as chaotic atoms that somehow coalesced into matter that ordered itself over aeons into a living substance.
en_example_chunks: Your trousers began as chaotic atoms / that somehow coalesced into matter / that ordered itself over aeons / into a living substance.
zh_example: 你的褲子最初不過是雜亂無序的原子，不知怎地凝聚成物質，經過漫長歲月自我組織成有生命的物質。

en_def: being a part of a whole; a component part
en_example: When the sun explodes, all the constituent atoms of this world will return to cold Chaos.
en_example_chunks: When the sun explodes, / all the constituent atoms of this world / will return to cold Chaos.
zh_example: 當太陽爆炸時，這個世界所有構成的原子都將回歸冰冷的混沌。

en_def: an indefinitely long period of time; an age
en_example: Matter ordered itself over aeons into the living substance that eventually became you.
en_example_chunks: Matter ordered itself / over aeons / into the living substance / that eventually became you.
zh_example: 物質經過漫長歲月自我整理，逐漸形成了最終成為你的那些生命物質。

en_def: to make something appear as if by magic; (conjure up) to produce an idea or image in the mind
zh_def: 施法變出；（conjure up）喚起、想像出
en_example: Whether Chaos brought life out of nothing or conjured it up in some other way, I don't know.
en_example_chunks: Whether Chaos brought life out of nothing / or conjured it up in some other way, / I don't know.
zh_example: 混沌究竟是無中生有帶來生命，還是用其他方式變出了生命，我並不知道。

en_def: to enclose or cover something, as if in a sheath
en_example: The cotton plant was woven into the handsome stuff that sheathes your lovely legs.
en_example_chunks: The cotton plant was woven / into the handsome stuff / that sheathes your lovely legs.
zh_example: 這株棉花被織成了包覆你美腿的那種考究布料。

en_def: certain to happen or to have a particular outcome; fated
en_example: Science today agrees that everything is destined to return to Chaos.
en_example_chunks: Science today agrees / that everything is destined / to return to Chaos.
zh_example: 當代科學也認同，萬物終將註定回歸混沌。

en_def: of, from, or like a god; relating to a deity
en_example: Was Chaos a god – a divine being – or simply a state of nothingness?
en_example_chunks: Was Chaos a god / – a divine being – / or simply a state of nothingness?
zh_example: 混沌究竟是一位神——一個神聖的存在——還是僅僅是一種虛無的狀態？

en_def: having no definite shape or structure
zh_def: 無形的；沒有固定形狀的
en_example: In the beginning, Chaos was entirely formless, with no shape or structure at all.
en_example_chunks: In the beginning, / Chaos was entirely formless, / with no shape or structure at all.
zh_example: 在最初，混沌完全沒有形狀，沒有任何結構。

en_def: to come into existence suddenly; to originate from
en_example: Two new beings sprang from the darkness the moment Chaos stirred.
en_example_chunks: Two new beings sprang / from the darkness / the moment Chaos stirred.
zh_example: 混沌一動，兩個新的存在便突然從黑暗中誕生。

en_def: to join or unite as a pair
en_example: Erebus and Nyx coupled, and from their union came Day and Light.
en_example_chunks: Erebus and Nyx coupled, / and from their union / came Day and Light.
zh_example: 厄瑞玻斯與倪克斯結合，他們的結合誕生了「日」與「光」。

en_def: happening or existing at the same time
en_example: Because there was no concept of time yet, several new beings appeared simultaneously.
en_example_chunks: Because there was no concept of time yet, / several new beings appeared / simultaneously.
zh_example: 因為當時還沒有時間的概念，好幾個新生的存在是同時出現的。

en_def: relating to the fundamental, basic forces of nature
en_example: The earliest deities were elemental forces rather than gods with real personalities.
en_example_chunks: The earliest deities / were elemental forces / rather than gods with real personalities.
zh_example: 最早的神祇是根本性的自然力量，而非具有真實人格的神明。

en_def: completely lacking or without something
en_example: These early beings were devoid of colour, character, or interest.
en_example_chunks: These early beings / were devoid of colour, / character, or interest.
zh_example: 這些早期的存在完全缺乏色彩、性格與趣味。

en_def: existing at or from the very beginning of time; primeval
en_example: The primordial deities existed long before any story could begin.
en_example_chunks: The primordial deities existed / long before / any story could begin.
zh_example: 太初的神祇早在任何故事得以展開之前，就已經存在。

en_def: to hover protectively or oppressively over something; to think deeply and gloomily
en_example: For ages, the primordial deities did nothing but brood over the silent, waiting world.
en_example_chunks: For ages, / the primordial deities did nothing but brood / over the silent, waiting world.
zh_example: 太初神祇長久以來只是沉沉地籠罩在這片寂靜、等待中的世界之上。

en_def: a person or thing that corresponds to or has the same function as another
en_example: Thalassa emerged as the female counterpart of Pontus, the sea.
en_example_chunks: Thalassa emerged / as the female counterpart / of Pontus, the sea.
zh_example: 塔拉薩是海神彭托斯的女性對應角色。

en_def: to come together to form one mass or whole
en_example: The clouds of the sky could coalesce into a shape that people might recognise.
en_example_chunks: The clouds of the sky / could coalesce / into a shape / that people might recognise.
zh_example: 天空中的雲朵能夠凝聚成人們可以辨認出的形體。

en_def: to move about in a state of turbulent agitation, as if boiling
en_example: Above the earth, the clouds rolled and seethed, not yet settled into any fixed form.
en_example_chunks: Above the earth, / the clouds rolled and seethed, / not yet settled / into any fixed form.
zh_example: 大地之上，雲朵翻騰滾動，尚未凝固成任何固定的形態。

en_def: offspring; descendants
en_example: Ouranos and Gaia gave the world yet more progeny – two distinctive sets of triplets.
en_example_chunks: Ouranos and Gaia / gave the world / yet more progeny – / two distinctive sets of triplets.
zh_example: 烏拉諾斯與蓋亞又為世界誕下了更多子嗣——兩組截然不同的三胞胎。

en_def: giving satisfaction or pleasure
zh_def: 令人滿意的；令人愉悅的
en_example: The union of Ouranos and Gaia was gratifyingly productive.
en_example_chunks: The union / of Ouranos and Gaia / was gratifyingly productive.
zh_example: 烏拉諾斯與蓋亞的結合，多產得令人滿意。

en_def: the process of beginning to grow or develop
en_example: The seeding of Gaia gave us meaning, a germination of thought into shape.
en_example_chunks: The seeding of Gaia / gave us meaning, / a germination of thought / into shape.
zh_example: 蓋亞的播種賦予了我們意義，那是思想成形的萌發。

en_def: to join together; to combine
en_example: In the creation of and conjoining with Ouranos, her son and now her husband, Gaia unwound the ribbon of life.
en_example_chunks: In the creation of / and conjoining with Ouranos, / her son and now her husband, / Gaia unwound the ribbon of life.
zh_example: 在創造並與烏拉諾斯——她的兒子、如今也是丈夫——結合的過程中，蓋亞展開了生命的緞帶。

en_def: to undo or release something that has been wound; to unravel
en_example: Gaia unwound the ribbon of life that runs all the way to human history and our own very selves.
en_example_chunks: Gaia unwound / the ribbon of life / that runs all the way / to human history and our own very selves.
zh_example: 蓋亞展開了那條一路延伸到人類歷史、乃至於我們自身的生命緞帶。

en_def: certain to happen or become something in the future
en_example: These twelve were destined to become the Second Order of divine beings.
en_example_chunks: These twelve / were destined / to become the Second Order / of divine beings.
zh_example: 這十二位註定將成為神界的「第二秩序」。

en_def: a variation or adjustment in tone, pitch, or expression
en_example: The three Cyclopes gave their father sky a whole new range of expressions and modulations.
en_example_chunks: The three Cyclopes / gave their father sky / a whole new range / of expressions and modulations.
zh_example: 三個獨眼巨人為他們的天空父親帶來了一整套全新的表情與聲調變化。

en_def: extremely ugly or unpleasant
en_example: The Hecatonchires were as hideous, fierce, violent and powerful as anything that had yet been released into being.
en_example_chunks: The Hecatonchires were / as hideous, fierce, violent and powerful / as anything / that had yet been released into being.
zh_example: 百手巨人如同已誕生之物中最醜陋可怕、兇猛、暴烈而強大的存在。

en_def: a sense of disgust and loathing
en_example: Ouranos was revolted by them; his revulsion was rooted in fear.
en_example_chunks: Ouranos was revolted by them; / his revulsion / was rooted in fear.
zh_example: 烏拉諾斯對他們感到厭惡；而這股厭惡的根源其實是恐懼。

en_def: to take great pleasure or pride in something
en_example: Ouranos could fill the heavens with flashes of lightning and crashing thunder. He gloried in the noise and spectacle.
en_example_chunks: Ouranos could fill the heavens / with flashes of lightning and crashing thunder. / He gloried / in the noise and spectacle.
zh_example: 烏拉諾斯能讓天空充滿閃電與轟雷。他陶醉於這番聲勢與奇觀之中。

en_def: a violent trembling or shivering movement
en_example: The second set of triplets Gaia bore sent even greater shudders through him and all who saw them.
en_example_chunks: The second set of triplets / Gaia bore / sent even greater shudders / through him and all who saw them.
zh_example: 蓋亞所生的第二組三胞胎，讓他與所有見過他們的人更加不寒而慄。

en_def: strong, healthy, and full of energy
en_example: AEGAEON the sea goat was sometimes also called BRIAREOS the vigorous one.
en_example_chunks: AEGAEON the sea goat / was sometimes also called / BRIAREOS the vigorous one.
zh_example: 海山羊埃該翁，有時也被稱為「精力充沛者」布里阿瑞俄斯。
